description: Code-switching evaluation protocol for NepTTS-Bench. Evaluates synthesis quality at Nepali-English language boundaries within utterances — voice quality, speaker identity, phonological correctness, and transition smoothness across switch points.

notes.usage: Run on the code-switching test set (50 utterances with annotated switch points). Evaluate each dimension independently. This protocol complements the robustness tests in stability/ by focusing specifically on language-boundary phenomena.
notes.evaluation: Code-switching quality is critical for natural bilingual Nepali-English speech. The system must maintain voice identity and produce smooth transitions at language boundaries while applying appropriate phonology (Nepali vs English) to each segment.
notes.reference: Test utterances drawn from robustness tests (../stability/nepali_robustness_tests.json) and conversational corpus entries with annotated language boundaries.

test_set.description: 50 utterances with annotated Nepali-English switch points for evaluating code-switching quality.
sources: Conversational corpus entries with English loanwords and phrases

annotations.switch_points: Character/word index where language switches from Nepali to English or vice versa
annotations.expected_phonology: Per-segment annotation: whether English words should use Nepali phonology (nativized loanwords) or English phonology (proper nouns, technical terms)
annotations.segment_boundaries_ms: Expected approximate timestamp boundaries for each language segment (generated from reference alignment)

voice_quality_at_boundaries.description: Perceptual speech quality measured in windows around language switch points. Quality should not degrade at boundaries compared to segment interiors.

measurement.boundary_window: 500ms window centered on each switch point
measurement.interior_window: 500ms window at the midpoint of each language segment

voice_quality_at_boundaries.notes: Negative delta (boundary worse than interior) is the failure mode. Positive delta is acceptable.

speaker_identity_across_switch.description: Speaker embedding similarity between Nepali and English segments within the same utterance. The voice should sound like the same speaker in both languages.
speaker_identity_across_switch.measurement: Extract speaker embeddings from Nepali-only and English-only segments of each utterance. Compute cosine similarity between the two embeddings.

phonology_correctness.description: Whether English words/phrases use the correct phonological system based on context. Nativized loanwords (e.g., 'computer' used in everyday Nepali) should use Nepali phonology. Proper nouns and technical terms may use English phonology.
phonology_correctness.evaluation_method: Human annotator evaluation on the full 50-utterance test set. Each English segment is rated as using correct or incorrect phonology based on the annotated expected_phonology field.

nativized_loanwords.description: English words commonly used in Nepali with Nepali phonology (e.g., 'bus' → 'बस', 'computer' → 'कम्प्युटर')

proper_nouns.description: Names, places, organizations that retain English pronunciation

phrases.description: Multi-word English phrases embedded in Nepali sentences
phrases.expected: English phonology with natural Nepali prosodic framing

transition_smoothness.description: F0 (fundamental frequency) continuity across switch points. Abrupt pitch discontinuities at language boundaries indicate poor transition quality.

measurement.f0_extraction: CREPE or PYIN on voiced segments
measurement.window: 100ms before and after each switch point
measurement.metric: Maximum F0 jump at switch point boundary

transition_smoothness.notes: Natural speech may have some F0 variation at language boundaries, but discontinuous jumps > 50Hz indicate synthesis artifacts rather than natural prosodic variation.

procedure: Generate audio for all 50 code-switching test utterances
Segment each utterance into Nepali and English portions using annotated switch points
Compute DNSMOS on boundary windows (500ms around switch points) and interior windows
Extract speaker embeddings from Nepali-only and English-only segments per utterance
Compute per-utterance speaker embedding similarity across languages
Extract F0 contours across switch point boundaries and measure maximum jumps
Conduct human phonology correctness evaluation on all 50 utterances
Run identical pipeline on teacher (Gemini) output for baseline comparison
Report per-metric results with pass/fail determination

aggregate_reporting.description: How to report code-switching evaluation results.
aggregate_reporting.pass_rate: Percentage of utterances meeting all 'pass' thresholds across all metrics
aggregate_reporting.minimum_rate: Percentage of utterances meeting all 'minimum' thresholds
visualizations: DNSMOS boundary vs interior scatter plot
F0 contour plots across switch points (overlay of multiple utterances)
Phonology correctness by category bar chart
